section.heading: Requirements for purchasing federally auctioned vessels

subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.chapeau: To be eligible to purchase a covered vessel from the Federal Government, a person shall provide proof of—

paragraph.content: liability insurance for the operator of such covered vessel;
financial resources sufficient to cover maintenance costs of such covered vessel; and
with respect to a covered vessel requiring documentation under chapter 121, an admiralty bond or stipulation.

subsection.heading: Covered Vessel Defined.—
subsection.chapeau: In this section, the term “covered vessel” means—

paragraph.content: a government owned vessel disposed of in accordance with this part and section 548 of title 40;

a vessel seized or forfeited pursuant to any law, and auctioned by the Federal Government, including a vessel seized or forfeited pursuant to section 7301 or 7302 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986; or
a fishing vessel seized or forfeited pursuant to section 310 of the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act (16 U.S.C. 1860).

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 118–159, div. C, title XXXV, § 3532(a), Dec. 23, 2024, 138 Stat. 2312.)

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: Sections 7301 and 7302 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, referred to in subsec. (b)(2), are classified to sections 7301 and 7302, respectively, of Title 26, Internal Revenue Code.
